text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Parenting & Childcare > Parenting Skills Classes * Disabilities Issues 3 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues African American Community At Risk Families Child Abuse Issues Disabilities Issues Divorce/Custody/Support Issues Domestic Violence Issues Fathers Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorders Hispanic/Latino Community Mothers Native American Community Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Sexual Assault Issues Substance Use Disorder Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details FACT OREGON PARENT TRAINING AND INFORMATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parent trainings, resources, information, and support to parents and caregivers on issues related to special education. FACT Help Line is available to answer questions, provide assistance with special educational concerns, and support families' preparation for IEP meetings. 2475 SE Ladd Avenue Suite 430 Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Families or professionals supporting a child experiencing disability ages 0-26 Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (888) 988-3228 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK MID-OREGON REGION OFFICE EDUCATION AND TRAINING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides training on topics such as collaborative problem solving, mental health first aid, self-care, trauma-informed care, and family-led crisis planning. 4263 Commercial Street SE Suite 300 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Professionals, volunteers, staff, community partners, youth, mentors, peer support, and family members Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 709-1366 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FACT OREGON PARENT TRAINING AND INFORMATION OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK EDUCATION AND TRAINING
